insights.title: Oscillator responds to MIDI note input and will retune itself
insights.summary: When a `core.oscillator` node receives a MIDI message, it tunes itself to the incoming note frequency. If you want to use it as a fixed-frequency LFO or modulation source, wrap it inside a `container.no_midi` node to prevent note messages from affecting its frequency.
insights.verify_hint: Test in a scriptnode synth context: oscillator without no_midi wrapper should retune on note-on.

insights.title: core.oscillator is monophonic — use container.multi for stereo
insights.summary: Like most oscillator nodes in scriptnode, `core.oscillator` operates on a single channel. To produce stereo output, place two instances inside a `container.multi` which automatically splits the processing per channel.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm core.oscillator is single-channel; verify container.multi splits per channel.

insights.title: Oscillator uptime counter can blow up after extended playback
insights.summary: A bug was reported where a `core.oscillator` running on the second channel caused the internal uptime counter to overflow/explode after several minutes of continuous playback, producing an audio artefact. The issue was reproduced inside a modchain context as well.
insights.verify_hint: Check if uptime counter overflow bug has been fixed in recent HISE commits.

insights.title: Build a hand-drawn oscillator by feeding a table into the oscillator
insights.summary: A custom waveform oscillator can be created by connecting a user-editable table to the oscillator node. The table data drives the waveform shape, allowing users to draw arbitrary waveforms at runtime. See aaronventure's snippet (tid:12679) for a complete implementation with UI and smoothing.
insights.verify_hint: Verify that core.oscillator accepts external table data to define the waveform shape.

insights.title: Amplitude-modulating an oscillator at audio rate requires a frame context
insights.summary: When modulating an oscillator's amplitude at very high speeds (audio-rate AM), the modulation will only update once per buffer unless the processing is moved into a frame-processing context. Use `container.frame2_block` or similar to get sample-accurate modulation. Additionally, compile the patch before checking the CPU overhead.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm that oscillator AM without frame processing produces stepped/staircase artefacts at audio rates.

insights.title: Randomise oscillator start phase per voice using a polyphonic delay node
insights.summary: To avoid phase-locked stacking of multiple oscillator voices, a polyphonic C++ node can apply a small random delay offset to each voice on note-on. Christoph confirmed that random phase offset is straightforward to implement in scriptnode using a small snippet.
insights.verify_hint: Christoph posted a HiseSnippet for this pattern in tid:12679 (content truncated in cache).
